_general.notes:
READ-ONLY / UNGATED. Observation cannot mutate host state, so every action on this surface — ps / ports / units / containers — is READ-ONLY and UNGATED: any actor may call them without a rationale. (The gated `exec` action was RETIRED from this surface in corpos-substrate-topology T6 — host command execution is host-loop work owned by the agent loop natively, not the shared substrate.)

STRUCTURED ROWS, NOT SHELL TEXT. Each action returns typed rows (e.g. a port row carries proto/state/local_addr/local_port/pid/process), parsed from the host tool's output (/proc, ss, systemctl, podman/docker) by a pure parser. A model reconstructs an inventory by joining these rows rather than scraping raw text, and the parsers are unit-tested against captured real output.

FAIL-SOFT. sys.containers probes podman and docker independently and contributes rows from whichever is present (an absent runtime is simply omitted). sys.ports / sys.units require their one tool (ss / systemctl) and error if it is missing.
_general.purpose: Owned system surface. Read-only introspection of live host state (processes, listening ports, systemd-user units, containers) — the owned, substrate-native counterpart to the harness Bash tool's introspection half. Returns structured rows rather than raw shell text.

containers.notes: podman ps --format json (a JSON array) and docker ps --format '{{json .}}' (newline-delimited objects) are parsed into a unified row shape. An absent runtime contributes no rows and is omitted from runtimes_queried. See go/internal/sys/testdata/INTROSPECTION_CONTRACT.md.

params.description: Only running containers (default false → include stopped via -a).
params.name: running_only

containers.purpose: List containers from podman and docker, each row tagged with its runtime. Read-only and ungated; fail-soft when a runtime is absent.

returns.description: containers (rows of {runtime, id, image, names, state, status, ports}), count, and runtimes_queried (which runtimes were probed).

errors.condition: the ss tool is not installed
errors.message: sys.ports: ss not found on PATH

ports.notes: Backed by `ss -tulnpH`. The owning pid/process comes from ss's users:((...)) column, present only for the caller's own sockets without root. The address column is split on its last ':' so IPv6 and scoped addresses parse. See go/internal/sys/testdata/INTROSPECTION_CONTRACT.md.

params.description: Restrict to a protocol: tcp or udp.
params.name: proto

ports.purpose: List listening TCP and bound UDP sockets (ss-equivalent), with the owning pid/process when visible. Read-only and ungated.

returns.description: ports (rows of {proto, state, local_addr, local_port, pid, process}) and count.

ps.notes: Self-defined (the harness has no process tool). /proc/<pid>/stat is parsed by splitting comm at the last ')'; rss_kb = rss_pages * pagesize/1024. command is the full cmdline (space-joined), or [comm] for kernel threads. See go/internal/sys/testdata/INTROSPECTION_CONTRACT.md.

params.description: Only include processes whose command contains this substring.
params.name: contains

params.description: Cap the number of rows returned (0 = all). Rows are ordered by pid.
params.name: limit

ps.purpose: Enumerate live processes by reading /proc directly (no ps dependency). Read-only and ungated.

returns.description: processes (rows of {pid, ppid, state, command, rss_kb}) and count.

errors.condition: the systemctl tool is not installed
errors.message: sys.units: systemctl not found on PATH

units.notes: Backed by `systemctl --user list-units --type=<type> -o json`. Self-defined. See go/internal/sys/testdata/INTROSPECTION_CONTRACT.md.

params.description: Only active units (default false → include inactive via --all).
params.name: active_only

units.purpose: List systemd-user units. Read-only and ungated.

returns.description: units (rows of {unit, load, active, sub, description}) and count.
